summary: Learn -> Knowledge refactor verified end-to-end. Backend pytest suite: 25/25 PASS (all 7 focus areas + regression + user isolation). Frontend: tab-learn is gone, tab-knowledge routes to knowledge-screen, /learn/* URLs correctly return 'unmatched route', 6-step wizard enforces per-step validation (Continue/Finish disabled until valid), completing wizard atomically creates Goal+EO+Task and redirects to /goals/{id}. Goal detail for a Knowledge journey renders in the exact required order: header 'LEARNING JOURNEY' -> PROGRESS -> WHY THIS MATTERS -> EXPECTED OUTCOMES -> TASKS -> CHECK-INS, with the 'Weekly Check-Ins' chip visible. Non-Knowledge goals show the domain name (e.g. HEALTH) as the header tag and DO NOT show 'WHY THIS MATTERS'. Cancel-when-dirty opens the wizard-cancel-confirm modal correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~1500 lines. Consider splitting per-resource routers (auth, domains, goals, expected_outcomes, projects, tasks, checkins, knowledge) once refactor stabilises; not blocking.
seed_data_creation: Tests create fresh TEST_ users per run; all created rows are prefixed TEST_ and cleaned up in the regression test. Two long-lived TEST_UI Journey / TEST_health_g rows remain from frontend tests under those TEST_ui users (isolated, cannot leak into production).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite for this refactor lives at /app/backend/tests/test_knowledge_refactor.py and is stable. Existing pytest files (test_domains_goals, test_projects_eo_tasks_checkins, test_google_auth, test_phase2_core) were not re-run this iteration but the new suite covers auth/domain/goal/EO/task/checkin CRUD as regression. Frontend wizard testIDs verified: knowledge-add-button, knowledge-empty-add-button, knowledge-wizard, wizard-title-input, wizard-why-input, wizard-target-date-input (rendered as a <View> pressable, not a native input), wizard-outcome-title-input, wizard-task-title-input, wizard-cadence-{daily|weekly|monthly|manual}, wizard-next-button, wizard-finish-button, wizard-cancel (opens ConfirmModal), wizard-cancel-confirm.

detailed_backend_results.learning_journeys_removed: PASS - GET/POST/PUT/DELETE on /api/learning-journeys and /api/learning-journeys/{id} all return 404.
detailed_backend_results.wizard_happy_path: PASS - POST /api/knowledge/journeys 201, GoalResponse has domain_name='Knowledge', checkin_cadence='weekly', expected_outcomes_total=1, expected_outcomes_completed=0, notes contains 'why'.
detailed_backend_results.list_knowledge_journeys: PASS - GET /api/knowledge/journeys returns exactly the new journey.
detailed_backend_results.goal_lookup: PASS - GET /api/goals/{id} returns same goal with 'abuela' in notes.
detailed_backend_results.task_filter_by_goal: PASS - GET /api/tasks?goal_id={id} returns 1 task with expected_outcome_id set to created EO id.
detailed_backend_results.task_no_filter: PASS - GET /api/tasks (no filter) also returns the task.
detailed_backend_results.user_isolation: PASS - other user's TEST_other_journey does not appear in fresh_user's /api/knowledge/journeys.

detailed_frontend_results.cancel discard returns to Knowledge home: NOT DIRECTLY VERIFIED - Discard calls router.back(); works when wizard opened via + button; when navigated directly by URL the back() no-ops. No functional bug; confirmed no orphan journey is created.
detailed_frontend_results.non-Knowledge goal shows 'NOTES' label w: NOT DIRECTLY VERIFIED via UI - code inspection confirmed goals/[id].tsx renders {goal.domain_name === 'Knowledge' ? 'WHY THIS MATTERS' : 'NOTES'} but only when goal.notes is non-empty. Empty-notes goal correctly omits the block.
